programmes.show_description: Featuring Dry Cleaning, shame, Jessy Blakemore & DUG. Hosted by Annie MacManus, Huw Stephens and MayKay.
Banner is met with a surprise, as the feud with the Duttons takes an unexpected turn.
Programmes start at 19:00.
Esther struggles to contain her renewed grief for Curtis before her emotions boil over and she lashes out at Marty.
Lacey and David are headed out of the hospital, unpacking Jo ending her engagement when Lacey bumps into Holden, a survivor from the train crash.
Jacqui Hurley is joined by Jamie Heaslip, Simon Zebo and Stephen Ferris for live coverage of Ireland v Wales from the Aviva Stadium. KO 8.10pm
A quiet and unassuming family man slowly reveals his true character after his house is burgled by two petty thieves. Crime drama, starring Bob Odenkirk and Connie Nielsen.

Programmes Start at 12:05.
Abigail's at a loose end waiting for her arm to recover as Mali tries to look out for her.
Remi's avoiding Levi's calls, refusing his referral, and calls a meeting with Eddie. Remi explains to him that he's decided to pull the plug on the label.
Eden and Cash are navigating the logistics of Remi staying with them long term, only made more complex as John sticks his nose in.
A woman who has been a bridesmaid too many times meets a handsome videographer at her cousin's wedding. Romantic drama, starring Rachel Boston and Paul Campbell.
It's Week 7, and it is time for the sewers to take on lingerie with pyjama blouses, transformed shapewear and bustier dresses.
Dogs and Bears and Minks: The team get a 911 call on a bear in a hot tub; Frank questions his night with Dolores.
Cows and Raccoons: Frank and Shred respond to a call of a distressed cow at a fraternity house; Patel learns a wholesome detail about Victoria's personal life.
The latest news and updates from the UEFA Champions League.

Former CIA agent Bryan Mills fights to rescue his ex-wife from an Albanian gangster whose son he killed. .Action thriller sequel, starring Liam Neeson and Famke Janssen.
